description:
You are a platform engineer looking after the company’s CI/CD pipelines.  
    Some careless commits left “*.tmp” configuration files scattered under

        /home/user/pipeline_configs/

    Your job is to clean the tree up with a small batch operation that uses ONLY
    “find” piped into “xargs” (plus the minimal helper commands needed for the
    renaming).  Do NOT loop explicitly in a shell script.

    Required end-state
    ------------------
    1. Every file whose **name ends in “.tmp”** inside
          /home/user/pipeline_configs/
       must be renamed in-place so that the same base name now ends in “.bak”.
       • Example: “…/staging.conf.tmp” → “…/staging.conf.bak”.

    2. Produce a plain-text log file
          /home/user/pipeline_configs/rename_report.log
       that lists every single rename, one rename per line, in the EXACT format:

          <old absolute path> -> <new absolute path>

       Example line (spaces are significant):
          /home/user/foo.tmp -> /home/user/foo.bak

       The log must contain ONLY these lines, sorted lexicographically
       (byte-wise ascending) by the old absolute path, and must end with a
       single trailing newline.

    3. After the operation:
       • No “*.tmp” files should exist anywhere in /home/user/pipeline_configs/.
       • The new “*.bak” files must contain exactly the same data that was in
         their “*.tmp” predecessors.
       • rename_report.log must match the format and ordering described above.

    Validation hints
    ----------------
    • You do NOT have sudo.  Stay entirely inside /home/user.
    • You may create short-lived helper scripts if you want, but they must not
      alter the required output format.
    • Use “find … -print0 | xargs ‑0 …” (or similar) so that paths with spaces
      will work, even though none exist right now.
    • Do not hard-code the file paths in your commands; rely on find-generated
      lists so the solution stays generic.

    When you are done, you can cat the log file to the terminal for human
    inspection, but the automated grader will check the file directly on disk.
truth:
<!--
        The initial filesystem state before the agent begins.
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <!-- Directory structure -->
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/                             (mode 755)
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/                        (mode 755)
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/app2/                        (mode 755)

        <!-- *.tmp files that MUST be renamed -->
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/staging.conf.tmp
            Content: "# temporary staging configuration\n"
            Mode   : 644
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/prod.conf.tmp
            Content: "# temporary production configuration\n"
            Mode   : 644
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/app2/dev.conf.tmp
            Content: "# temporary development configuration\n"
            Mode   : 644

        <!-- Files that must remain untouched -->
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/settings.conf
            Content: "# permanent config – do not touch\n"
            Mode   : 644
    </initial_files>

    <final_state>
        <!-- The three .tmp files above MUST be gone -->
        Non-existence:
            /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/staging.conf.tmp
            /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/prod.conf.tmp
            /home/user/pipeline_configs/app2/dev.conf.tmp

        <!-- Corresponding .bak files MUST exist with identical contents -->
        Files and expected contents:
            /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/staging.conf.bak
                "# temporary staging configuration\n"
            /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/prod.conf.bak
                "# temporary production configuration\n"
            /home/user/pipeline_configs/app2/dev.conf.bak
                "# temporary development configuration\n"

        <!-- Log file -->
        /home/user/pipeline_configs/rename_report.log
            Expected exact text (with a final newline character):
/home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/prod.conf.tmp -> /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/prod.conf.bak
/home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/staging.conf.tmp -> /home/user/pipeline_configs/app1/staging.conf.bak
/home/user/pipeline_configs/app2/dev.conf.tmp -> /home/user/pipeline_configs/app2/dev.conf.bak

    </final_state>
